questions.prompt: Select all that are true about a single slide of a fixed-size window one step to the right.
choices: Exactly one element leaves on the left and exactly one element enters on the right.
The shared middle elements are carried over without being re-added.
The running sum is repaired by subtracting the leaver and adding the enterer.
questions.explanation: One slide changes only the two boundary elements. The middle is reused, and the sum is repaired by subtracting the element that leaves and adding the element that enters.

questions.prompt: Which statements about the O(1) slide update are true?
choices: The cost per slide does not depend on k
You add the entering element and subtract the leaving element
You must re-sum all k elements each step
The leaving element is nums[right - k] when the right pointer is at index right
questions.explanation: The whole point is to avoid re-summing. The update is add-enter, subtract-leave, and the leaver is k behind the right pointer.

questions.prompt: Which of these are REQUIRED for a fixed-size window to be correct? (Select all that truly are. If none, select none.)
choices: The array must be sorted first
The window width must be a power of two
questions.explanation: None of these are required. A fixed-size window sum works on any integers in any order, including negatives, and any width.

questions.prompt: Put the fixed-size-window algorithm steps in order.
items: Sum the first k elements to seed the running total
Set best equal to the first window's sum
For each new right index, add the entering and subtract the leaving element
Update best if the current window sum is larger
correct_order: Sum the first k elements to seed the running total
Set best equal to the first window's sum
For each new right index, add the entering and subtract the leaving element
Update best if the current window sum is larger

questions.prompt: "Given an array of integers and a number k, find the maximum average of any contiguous subarray of length k." Which pattern(s) apply?
questions.explanation: "Contiguous subarray of length k" is the fixed-size sliding-window fingerprint. A two-pointer framing is a reasonable secondary lens since the window is bounded by two indices.

questions.prompt: Explain, in your own words, why the per-slide cost of a fixed-size window is O(1) even though the window contains k elements.
questions.actual_answer: Because sliding changes only the two boundary elements: the running sum is updated by adding the one element that enters on the right and subtracting the one that leaves on the left. The k-2 shared middle elements are carried over unchanged, so the work per slide is a constant two operations regardless of k.
questions.rubric: Full credit: identifies that only two elements change (one enters, one leaves) and the shared middle is reused, so the update is constant work independent of k. Partial: mentions add/subtract but not the reuse of the middle. Low: restates O(1) without the mechanism.
